factors.name: US Inflation Surge (Oil-Driven)
factors.summary: Treasury yields climbing on persistent inflation concerns; WTI holding near $97.50 as Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed. Bloomberg reports global bond yields rising amid inflation angst and energy shock.

factors.name: Fed Policy Ambiguity
factors.summary: Fed's Barr and Williams remain noncommittal on June/future hikes; Barr hasn't decided on June FOMC move. Policy described as 'in a good place' but inflation uncertainty noted as key risk.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength (DXY >99.00)
factors.summary: DXY climbs above 99.00 for fifth consecutive day on strong retail sales (+0.5% MoM) and Fed hawkish shift. Headwind for Dow multinationals with ~50% international revenue exposure.

factors.name: Trump-Xi Trade Summit (Positive Optics)
factors.summary: China agrees to buy 200 Boeing jets and double-digit billions in US agricultural products. Trade truce extends, tariff uncertainty remains but tone constructive. USTR Greer signals 'lot of success' in rebalancing.

factors.name: Boeing Disappointment (Dow Component)
factors.summary: Boeing stock headed for steepest 6-month drop as China order (200 jets) fell far short of expectations (500+). Price-weighted Dow hit hard by individual heavyweight underperformance.

factors.name: Global Growth Weakness & Supply Chain Stress
factors.summary: South Korea's KOSPI reversed from 8,046 record high to -2% on Iran/Hormuz concerns. New Zealand manufacturing PMI collapsed to 50.5 from 52.8; freight disruptions cited. Iran war resolution at impasse.

factors.name: Japan Wholesale Inflation Shock (PPI +4.9% YoY)
factors.summary: Japan April PPI surged 4.9% vs 3.0% expected (largest since 2014), import prices up 17.5% YoY on Iran war energy costs. Supports BOJ June rate hike case; global inflation pressure broadening.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran, Hormuz Closure)
factors.summary: Trump signals impatience with Iran; Strait of Hormuz effectively closed. Oil elevated but risk premium embedded; uncertainty on resolution weighing on cyclical confidence and shipping indices.

factors.name: Dow Earnings Mix / Forward Guidance Pending
factors.summary: Limited component earnings data in this window. Achieve Life Sciences beat Q1 EPS, but no major Dow heavyweights (UNH, JPM, CAT, GS, HD) reported. Guidance will be critical test for cyclical outlook.

factors.name: Real Yields Rising (Rate Hikes Priced In)
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury yields climbing on inflation and Fed uncertainty. Higher real yields typically headwind for equities, particularly dividend-heavy Dow; rate-sensitive financials face compression.

factors.name: AI Sector Momentum Fading
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports 'Record-breaking global stock rally driven by AI optimism stalled in Asia; sentiment souring.' Nvidia earnings expected but sentiment already cooling; Nvidia a top Dow/broader influence.

factors.name: Copper Retreat & Metals Weakness
factors.summary: Copper extending retreat from record highs as inflation and stronger dollar weigh. Industrial input cost relief fading; bad signal for CAT, DE, MMM guidance and capex cycles.

screening.narrative: The Dow faces cross-currents: Trump-Xi trade optimism (China ag + Boeing orders) is offset by broadening global inflation shock (Japan PPI +4.9%, Treasuries rising, dollar strong), supply chain stress from Iran/Hormuz closure, and fading AI/growth momentum. Boeing's disappointing order miss has dampened index enthusiasm. Fed policy ambiguity (Barr/Williams sitting on hands into June) and climbing real yields are headwinds for cyclicals and dividend payers. The 0.12% 4h / 0.23% 24h move is muted, suggesting consolidation rather than conviction in either direction.
catalysts_ahead: US Retail Sales (already released; +0.5% MoM beat, bullish signal for consumers but stoked inflation fears)
ISM Manufacturing PMI (due Fri–Mon window; critical for Dow industrial exposure)
ISM Services PMI (due Fri–Mon window; services-heavy Dow exposure test)
Dow component earnings (UNH, JPM, GS, HD, CAT, V, MSFT, BA—forward guidance critical)
FOMC Meeting / Dot Plot (mid-May; rate-expectations hinge point)
China macro data (latest PMI, industrial output; affects CAT, BA demand)
CPI/PCE inflation prints (May releases; will confirm or deny peak-inflation narrative)
Japan BOJ action (Jun rate hike likely; yen intervention risk)
Geopolitical resolution on Iran/Hormuz (if resolved, relief rally; if escalates, oil spike)
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Fed signals imminent rate cuts (risk-off reversal into safe havens), (2) Iran conflict resolves and oil drops sharply (inflation fears ease, real yields fall), (3) Dow heavyweights (CAT, JPM, GS) deliver upside earnings + bullish guidance (cyclical rebound), (4) China PMI or stimulus surprises to upside (rescues growth narrative), or (5) Trump-Xi tariff deal is fully resolved with zero new tariffs (trade risk-off fully priced).
screening.entry_guidance: Move is mixed and early-stage; market consolidating between competing narratives (trade optimism vs. inflation/rate concerns). Await ISM PMI and first earnings set (JPM, GS, UNH) before committing. Short resistance at 50,200–50,400 on rate-rise concerns; long support at 49,800 if inflation narrative softens. Confidence limited by conflicting signals and undecided Fed stance—do not chase either direction.
